product_name: Iron with B Vitamins and Vitamin C (Ferrochel)
manufacturer: Herbs of Gold (amber bottle, white cap)
corpus_notes: Highest nutritional complexity label in corpus. Multiple training values: (1) Iron as bisglycinate with trademark 'Ferrochel' — tests trademark stripping for canonical name lookup. (2) Multiple B vitamins as active/phosphorylated forms: Thiamine HCl → B1, Riboflavin sodium phosphate → B2, Pyridoxal 5-phosphate → B6 (active form — tier 1). (3) Levomefolate glucosamine (Quatrefolic) → 5-MTHF — active folate, tests complex trademarked ingredient. (4) Mecobalamin = methylcobalamin → B12. (5) Calcium ascorbate → Vitamin C. (6) All have compound and elemental values. (7) Trademarked ingredients: Ferrochel, Quatrefolic — parser must identify canonical nutrient regardless of trademark.

expected_entries.name: Iron (II) glycinate (bisglycinate)
expected_entries.form_rationale: Ferrochel ferrous bisglycinate — highest bioavailability iron form, gentlest on GI tract

expected_entries.name: Riboflavin sodium phosphate
expected_entries.form_rationale: Riboflavin-5-phosphate is the active coenzyme form

expected_entries.name: Pyridoxal 5-phosphate monohydrate
expected_entries.form_rationale: P5P is the active coenzyme form — superior to pyridoxine HCl, especially for MTHFR variants

expected_entries.name: Levomefolate glucosamine
expected_entries.form_rationale: Quatrefolic (5-MTHF) is the bioactive form — bypasses MTHFR enzyme, superior for ~40% population with MTHFR polymorphism

expected_entries.name: Mecobalamin
expected_entries.form_rationale: Methylcobalamin (mecobalamin) is the active, neurologically-preferred B12 form
expected_entries.alias_note: mecobalamin = methylcobalamin — ensure alias lookup covers this

clinical_notes.target_use: Iron deficiency with B-vitamin support (energy, methylation)
clinical_notes.mthfr_note: 5-MTHF + methylcobalamin combination is specifically beneficial for MTHFR C677T/A1298C variants — clinically important to note in form quality assessment
clinical_notes.drug_interactions_flag: Iron bisglycinate may still interact with quinolone/tetracycline antibiotics and levothyroxine — take 2 hours apart. Vitamin C enhances iron absorption (already co-formulated here). B6 as P5P — caution at high doses in peripheral neuropathy.
